ops.title: v0.8.0 — ccx code grep -i/-w runs on PATH-resolved rg (grep fallback); guard pack rewrites blocked commands in place, gated by ccx_supports probe
ops.body:
Shipped 2026-07-08 as v0.8.0 (v0.7.0 was the ccx web release earlier the same day).

- `ccx code grep -i/-w` dispatches to `internal/ripgrep`: PATH-resolved `rg --json --fixed-strings`, falling back to system `grep -rnFI` (filesystem-validated line parsing — paths must be regular files; hidden/binary skipped; `.gitignore` NOT applied, disclosed in an engine note). Both engines reshape into tilth's grep render format so `annotateGrep` anchors + `Cap` budgets apply unchanged. Positional paths ride behind `--`. tilth cannot do case-folding (pinned third-party binary, literal matcher) — that's why the second engine exists.
- The dispatch special-case lives in BOTH `internal/cli/run.go` and `internal/proxy/proxy.go` (the MCP/codeexec path does NOT go through cli dispatch — proxy.call() mirrors it; keep them in sync when adding engine routes).
- Guard pack (`plugin/hooks/`) is rewrite-first: mappable shapes rewrite via `updatedInput` + note (grep→code grep, bare git diff/show/log -p + jj diff→ccx vcs, curl/wget page dumps→web read --full, large Reads→limit=100); unmappable shapes keep the block fallback. [[capt-hook-cc-context-wiring]]
- `common.py:ccx_supports(*subcmd, flag=)` (--help probe, functools.cache) gates rewrites needing a newer binary than a consumer's brew ccx — the brew-first skew ([[brew-first-installer]]) is permanent, so new-flag rewrites must stay gated. Gated shapes are tested ONLY in pytest with the probe monkeypatched (+cache_clear), never in inline tests={} (environment-dependent).
- `LITERAL_SAFE` must gate grep rewrites on EVERY engine: raw grep patterns are BRE regex (`.` is a wildcard), so metachar patterns can never rewrite to a literal matcher; dot patterns rewrite but the note discloses literal-dot semantics.
- `internal/vcs`: jj-native classification is exact-match `@`/`@-`/`@+` only (bare `@` must never hit `git rev-parse` — it resolves to HEAD, which is jj's `@-`); embedded-`@` strings (`release@1` vs `main@origin`) are lexically indistinguishable — classify by attempting git resolution, falling through to jj. Ranges stay lexical (git can't rev-parse a range).
- Hook test runners: `uvx capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks test` (pack dir on the global flag, NOT positional) + `PYTHONPATH=plugin uv run --project ../captain-hook --with pytest pytest plugin/hooks/`.
